rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Otice Of Motion And Motion For Allocation Of Defendants Attorneys Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KATHRYN DEVINCENZI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ALLOCATION OF DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES is continued to October 14, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Award Of Common Benefit Attorneys Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Benjamin C. Graves In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR AWARD OF COMMON BENEFIT ATTORNEYS FEES is OFF CALENDAR. Papers filed on July 22, 2022 did not include a required Notice of Motion. Plaintiff to file a complete motion including all required documents in one submission and set it for hearing on October 14, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Award Of Common Benefit Attorneys Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Benjamin C. Graves In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AWARD OF COMMON BENEFIT ATTORNEYS FEES is OFF CALENDAR. Papers filed on July 25, 2022 included only Notice of Motion with no supporting documents and no express reference to the documents previously filed (exact title, date of the filing, etc). Plaintiff to file a complete motion including all required documents in one submission and set it for hearing on October 14, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZHIJUN WU VS. JIANBO ZHONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Proof of Service indicates e-service on July 5, 2022 for July 27, 2022 hearing. This is exactly 16 court days without additional time for e-service. CCP 1010.6. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
